title: lithocarpus apricus
intro: Lithocarpus apricus, also known as the apricot beech, is a type of evergreen tree native to China, characterized by its small, reddish-brown fruits and edible nuts. The medicinal properties of Lithocarpus apricus include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to improve cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and potentially help manage diabetes. Lithocarpus apricus is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
